systemStatus.headline: Sun Jul 19, 7 AM run. All sources fresh -- Toast Sat Jul 18 and Sling Jul 19 both landed and are parsed. THE WORLD CUP FINAL IS TODAY AND THE 'World Cup HQ 2026' CAMPAIGN ($20/day) IS STILL PAUSED -- 0 impressions Jul 18, confirmed live this run. Sat Jul 18 sales came in soft vs the prior Saturday: net $9,261.85, -7.4%, on 10% fewer guests and 19% fewer orders, though average check rose 14.5%. Labor 20.3% of net, above the 18-19% band. Today is staffed 15 employees / 16 shifts / 74.82 hrs, 0 uncovered.
systemStatus.detail:
Sun Jul 19 07:00 AM PT run.

TIMING: Unlike the 4 AM cycle, both feeds were available this run. Toast's Sat Jul 18 GROUP summary arrived 6:26 AM PT and Sling's Jul 19 schedule 7:01 AM PT. Sales and staffing below are ACTUALS, not carried-forward values. The Toast LOCATION-level email (labor $, void $, checks, dayparts, rooms) had not landed yet, so those fields remain null or derived.

TOAST (Sat Jul 18, group-level): net $9,261.85 / gross $9,536.85 / 361 guests / 190 orders / avg guest $25.66 / avg order $48.75 / labor 20.3% / discounts 2.9% / refunds $0 / 12 void items / 157 card payments. vs SDLW (Sat Jul 11): net -7.4%, gross -6.8%, guests -10.4%, orders -19.1%. The story is traffic, not spend -- 45 fewer orders and 42 fewer guests, but each order was worth $6.17 more. Labor at 20.3% is up 3.4 points from Jul 11's 16.9%, which on lower volume is the item to watch. One order was left open at close and Grant Danely was auto-clocked out, exactly as on Sat Jul 11 -- a recurring closeout pattern.

SLING (Sun Jul 19): 15 staff / 16 shifts / 74.82 hrs / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off. More bodies than Saturday (+2) on slightly fewer hours (-2.16). Seven of sixteen shifts start between 11:00 and 11:30A. Coverage thins after 6:30P -- five people carry the evening and the last shift ends 10:00P. If the Final drives an afternoon/evening crowd, the 6:30-10:00P block is the thin spot. The malformed 24-hour shift count dropped from two to one: Isael Salvador Narciso (Cook) 4:31A-4:31A persists; Julianna Ornelas's 6:33A-6:33A entry cleared, and her split today (Barista 6:30A-12:30P, Runner 12:30P-3:00P) is legitimate back-to-back scheduling, not a double-booking.

GOOGLE ADS (Jul 18, live): $55.26 / 6,933 impr / 155 clicks / CTR 2.24% / CPC $0.36 / conversions 0.00 (pending). Cheapest, most efficient day in the current series -- 23 more clicks for $13.36 less. But only 2 of 4 campaigns served. World Cup HQ 2026 ($20/day, Search) sat paused at 0 impressions on the eve of the Final, and Oldest SB Brewery on Tap ($10.80/day) remains paused with all asset groups policy-limited. PMax-2 carried 95% of impressions while flagged both 'Limited by budget' and policy-limited. Conversions have now read 0.00 for several consecutive runs; that is a tracking question, not a performance fact.

YELP (last 30d, live): 29.9k ad impressions (72% of 41.4k), 397 clicks, 525 page visits from ads (57%), 134 leads from ads (58% of 230). Billing Jul 1-18: $241.57, CPC $0.99, CPL $2.78, ~$13.42/day. Steady. Ad Settings flags audience size 'Limited' with control features raising CPC -- Yelp's CPC is roughly 3x Google's. Bonus budget remaining still not surfaced (5th run).

TRIPADVISOR: no ad performance report in 72h.

NOT REFRESHED THIS RUN (carried from the 4 AM cycle, scope of this job is Toast/Sling/Google/Yelp/TripAdvisor): reviews, reservations, Yelp inquiries, business profile, payroll. The 4 AM run recorded 1 unreplied review (Thuy Anh Le, The Brewhouse) and 2 confirmed 11:00 AM parties totaling 10 guests, one explicitly for the Final.

toast.source: GROUP-level Daily Performance Summary (The Brewhouse Santa Barbara Group, Sat Jul 18), email received Sun Jul 19 6:26 AM PT. LOCATION-level email (labor $, void $, dayparts, rooms, checks) had not landed at the 7 AM run. SDLW computed locally against the Sat Jul 11 GROUP email.
toast.note: SAT JUL 18 ACTUALS (group-level). Net $9,261.85 / Gross $9,536.85 / 361 guests / 190 orders / avg guest $25.66 / avg order $48.75. Labor 20.3% of net (~$1,880) -- above the 18-19% band and up 3.4 pts from Sat Jul 11's 16.9%. Discounts 2.9% (~$277), refunds $0, deferred $0, 12 void items, 157 card payments. ONE order left open at close and Grant Danely was auto-clocked out again (same as Sat Jul 11). vs SDLW (Sat Jul 11): net -$743.67 (-7.4%), gross -6.8%, guests -42 (-10.4%), orders -45 (-19.1%). Traffic was down but spend per visit was UP -- avg/order +$6.17 (+14.5%), avg/guest +$0.83 (+3.3%). Fewer, bigger tickets.

sling.note: TODAY (Sun Jul 19), email delivered 7:01 AM PT. 15 employees / 16 shifts / 74.82 hrs / 0 uncovered / 0 OT / 0 off. Heaviest headcount of the week (+2 employees, +2 shifts vs Sat Jul 18) but 2.16 fewer hours -- more people on shorter, tighter shifts, and 11:00-11:30A is the big stack (7 of 16 shifts start in that half hour). ONE malformed 24-hour shift remains: Isael Salvador Narciso (Cook) 4:31A-4:31A -- down from two yesterday; the Sat Jul 18 Julianna 6:33A-6:33A entry cleared. Julianna Ornelas works a legitimate split today (Barista 6:30A-12:30P then Runner 12:30P-3:00P), back-to-back rather than double-booked. Floor thins after 6:30P: only 5 people carry 6:30P-9:30P and the last shift ends 10:00P.

yelpAds.note: LIVE scrape Sun Jul 19 ~7:08 AM PT. Last 30d: 29.9k ad impressions (72% of 41.4k), 397 ad clicks, 525 page visits from ads (57% of 916), 134 leads from ads (58% of 230). Billing Jul 1-18: spend $241.57, CPC $0.99, CPL $2.78 -- running ~$13.42/day, essentially flat. Day-over-day the platform added $13.07 spend, 5 clicks, 2 page visits and 1 lead: steady, unremarkable. 12-mo ad contribution 1,111 ad leads vs 729 organic (60/40); forecast dropped to 98 from 106. Ad Settings pane reports audience size 'Limited' and warns that control features are restricting reach and raising CPC -- worth a look given CPC is 3x Google's $0.36. Bonus budget remaining is STILL not surfaced anywhere on the dashboard (5th consecutive run) -- treat as unavailable, not zero. Yelp Inbox shows 15 unread.
